basic_info.title: Drill Dozer
basic_info.review: Drill Dozer is one of the best GBA games with brilliant design and fantastic gameplay, featuring unique drill-focused mechanics that set it apart from other platformers.

alternative_names: Screw Breaker Gōshin Drillero (スクリューブレイカー轟振どりるれろ)

play_today.platform: Wii U Virtual Console (via RetroArch / Emulation)
play_today.details: The most accessible version for modern play. Preserves the original GBA rumble functionality when played with compatible controllers. The Wii U eShop has been discontinued as of March 2023, but the game remains available through existing purchases.

recommended_patches.name: Rumble Patch for EZ3in1
recommended_patches.description: Enables rumble functionality on EZ3in1 flash carts that cannot directly play Drill Dozer with rumble.

recommended_patches.name: Game Boy Player Rumble Patch
recommended_patches.description: Unlocks the Game Boy Player rumble feature on GBA and adds cartridge rumble support.

optimal_settings: Enable rumble support if using compatible controller
Use accurate timing for best compatibility
Enable rumble vibration if supported by device

play_today.details: The original release with built-in rumble pack functionality. This is the definitive version for collectors and enthusiasts who want the authentic experience with hardware rumble support.

description.synopsis: Jill, daughter of a bandit gang leader, pilots her mech Drill Dozer to retrieve the Red Diamond stolen from her family by the rival Skullkers gang while collecting four other diamonds that affect people and objects across the world.
key_features: Unique drill-based gameplay with clockwise/counter-clockwise rotation controls
Built-in rumble pack functionality activated during drilling sequences
17 massive stages filled with enemies, obstacles, and puzzles
Gear shift system that allows indefinite drilling after collecting sprockets
Treasure collection system to increase Jill's reputation as a thief
Unlockable outfits themed after Game Freak characters in English version
description.long_description: Drill Dozer is a 2005 action-platformer video game developed by Game Freak and published by Nintendo for the Game Boy Advance. Directed by Ken Sugimori, the first game he directed since Pulseman in 1994, it stars Jill and her mech Drill Dozer, which features a drill that can be rotated in both directions to fight enemies, drill into surfaces, and screw objects. The game was revealed at E3 2005 under its original title 'Screw Breaker' before being localized for international release. Development began after Pokémon Ruby and Sapphire when Game Freak had staff available who hadn't worked on the mainline Pokémon games. Sugimori wanted to create an action game that didn't use traditional combat, inspired by tokusatsu monsters that could drill through anything. The protagonist was designed as a little girl piloting a mech rather than a muscular male character, with her hair design inspired by roll cakes. The game features shoulder button controls for drilling direction, with rumble functionality added late in development at Nintendo's suggestion. It received generally favorable reviews and has been regarded as one of the best GBA games by multiple publications including Nintendo Power (GBA Game of the Year 2006), though it was a commercial failure due to coinciding with the Nintendo DS release and poor marketing, becoming a cult classic hidden gem.
legacy_and_impact: Regarded as one of the best Game Boy Advance games by multiple publications
Won GBA 'Game of the Show' at E3 2005 from Nintendo World Report
Featured as summonable character in Super Smash Bros. Brawl (2008)
Inspired Game Freak's later action game Giga Wrecker which shares similar essence
Demonstrated Game Freak's ability to create successful non-Pokémon titles
Preserved rumble functionality as a notable feature in Virtual Console releases
